description: This bar chart demonstrates the bake hardening (BH) response of 5 Fe-Al ferritic steels after standard paint baking treatment (2% pre-deformation + 170 °C holding for 20 min). The nominal compositions (wt.%) of the 5 alloys are: 1 = 7Al-0.002C-0.01Ti, 2 = 7Al-0.004C-0.02Nb, 3 = 8Al-0.003C-0.01Ti-0.01Nb, 4 = 7.5Al-0.004C-0.02Nb, 5 = 6.5Al-0.005C-0.01Ti-0.01Nb. White bars represent pre-BH yield strength (YS, unit MPa), pink bars represent post-BH YS, and blue dots represent the YS increment (ΔYS) after BH treatment. ΔYS stays around 80 MPa for all 5 alloys, showing consistent bake hardening effect. Alloy 3 exhibits the highest pre-BH YS (~420 MPa) and post-BH YS (~500 MPa) among all samples. This figure confirms that properly designed high-Al low-carbon ferritic lightweight steels can achieve stable ~80 MPa yield strength improvement via BH treatment, originating from interstitial C atoms locking dislocations in the ferritic matrix, which satisfies the mechanical requirement for automotive lightweight applications.
